𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄

THE ANCIENT COVENANT RECORD

Volume Fifteen — Part Two

The Book of Melakhim Bet

MELAKHIM BET

The Second Book of Kings

Chapters 8 through 13

Khaza'el — Yehu's Purge — Atalyahu — Yo'ash — Elisha's Death

PRIMARY MANUSCRIPT AUTHORITIES

No complete DSS manuscript for Melakhim Bet

Ethiopian Orit Ge'ez — Complete Canon

YHWH and The Creator only

COMPARATIVE NOTES AT END OF EVERY CHAPTER

DSS · ORIT GE'EZ · MASORETIC VARIANT · CRITICAL NOTE

YHWH / 𐤉𐤄𐤅𐤄 and The Creator only — no substitute titles

All personal names in original Hebrew form throughout

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 13 — YEHU TO ELISHA'S DEATH

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 8 The Shunammite's Land Restored — Khaza'el Smothers Ben-Hadad — Yehoram and Akhazyahu of Yehudah

1 Now Elisha had said to the woman whose son he had restored to life, Arise, and depart with your household, and sojourn wherever you can, for YHWH has called for a famine, and it will come upon the land for seven years.

2 So the woman arose and did according to the word of the man of Creator. She went with her household and sojourned in the land of the Pelishtim seven years.

3 And at the end of the seven years, when the woman returned from the land of the Pelishtim, she went to appeal to the king for her house and her land.

4 Now the king was talking with Gekhazi the servant of the man of Creator, saying, Tell me all the great things that Elisha has done.

5 And while he was telling the king how Elisha had restored the dead to life, behold, the woman whose son he had restored to life appealed to the king for her house and her land. And Gekhazi said, My lord, O king, here is the woman, and here is her son whom Elisha restored to life.

6 And when the king asked the woman, she told him. So the king appointed an official for her, saying, Restore all that was hers, together with all the produce of the fields from the day that she left the land until now.

7 Now Elisha came to Damascus. Ben-Hadad the king of Aram was sick. And when it was told him, The man of Creator has come here,

8 The king said to Khaza'el, Take a present with you and go to meet the man of Creator, and inquire of YHWH through him, saying, Shall I recover from this sickness?

9 So Khaza'el went to meet him, and took a present with him, all kinds of goods of Damascus, forty camel loads. When he came and stood before him, he said, Your son Ben-Hadad king of Aram has sent me to you, saying, Shall I recover from this sickness?

10 And Elisha said to him, Go, say to him, You shall certainly recover, but YHWH has shown me that he shall certainly die.

11 And he fixed his gaze and stared at him, until he was embarrassed. And the man of Creator wept.

12 And Khaza'el said, Why does my lord weep? He answered, Because I know the evil that you will do to the people of Yisra'EL. You will set on fire their fortresses, and you will kill their young men with the sword and dash in pieces their little ones and rip open their pregnant women.

13 And Khaza'el said, What is your servant, who is but a dog, that he should do this great thing? Elisha answered, YHWH has shown me that you are to be king over Aram.

14 Then he departed from Elisha and came to his master, who said to him, What did Elisha say to you? And he answered, He told me that you would certainly recover.

15 But the next day he took the bed cloth and dipped it in water and spread it over his face, till he died. And Khaza'el became king in his place.

16 In the fifth year of Yehoram the son of Akhav, king of Yisra'EL, when Yehoshafat was king of Yehudah, Yehoram the son of Yehoshafat king of Yehudah began to reign.

17 He was thirty-two years old when he became king, and he reigned eight years in Yerushalayim.

18 And he walked in the way of the kings of Yisra'EL, as the house of Akhav had done, for the daughter of Akhav was his wife. And he did what was evil in the sight of YHWH.

19 Yet YHWH was not willing to destroy Yehudah, for the sake of Dawid his servant, since he promised to give a lamp to him and to his sons forever.

20 In his days Edom revolted from the rule of Yehudah and set up a king of their own.

21 Then Yehoram passed over to Tza'ir with all his chariots and rose by night, and he and his chariot commanders struck the Edomites who had surrounded him, but his army fled home.

22 So Edom revolted from the rule of Yehudah to this day. Then Livnah revolted at the same time.

23 Now the rest of the acts of Yehoram, and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

24 And Yehoram slept with his fathers and was buried with his fathers in the city of Dawid. And Akhazyahu his son reigned in his place.

25 In the twelfth year of Yehoram the son of Akhav, king of Yisra'EL, Akhazyahu the son of Yehoram, king of Yehudah, began to reign.

26 Akhazyahu was twenty-two years old when he began to reign, and he reigned one year in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Athalyahu, the granddaughter of Omri king of Yisra'EL.

27 He also walked in the way of the house of Akhav and did what was evil in the sight of YHWH, as the house of Akhav had done, for he was son-in-law to the house of Akhav.

28 He went with Yehoram the son of Akhav to make war against Khaza'el king of Aram at Ramot-Gil'ad. And the Arameans wounded Yehoram.

29 And King Yehoram returned to be healed in Yizre'el of the wounds that the Arameans had given him at Ramah, when he fought against Khaza'el king of Aram. And Akhazyahu the son of Yehoram king of Yehudah went down to see Yehoram the son of Akhav in Yizre'el, because he was sick.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Elisha's weeping before Khaza'el — verse 11-12 — is one of the most emotionally charged prophetic moments in the Nevi'im. The prophet sees with covenant clarity the horror that Khaza'el will inflict on Yisra'EL and weeps over it even as he announces it.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 8 in full. The covenant continuity of the Davidic lamp — verse 19 — yet YHWH was not willing to destroy Yehudah, for the sake of Dawid his servant, since he promised to give a lamp to him and to his sons forever — is read as the covenant declaration that the Davidic promise operates independently of any individual king's faithfulness.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Elisha's answer to Khaza'el — verse 10 — go, say to him, you shall certainly recover, but YHWH has shown me that he shall certainly die — is not a contradiction. Ben-Hadad's illness would not kill him. Khaza'el would.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 12-13 — Elisha weeps because he knows the evil Khaza'el will do: fortresses burned, young men killed, children dashed, pregnant women ripped open. Khaza'el's response — what is your servant, who is but a dog, that he should do this great thing? — is not false modesty. He genuinely cannot conceive himself capable of such atrocities. Elisha's answer — YHWH has shown me that you are to be king — is the covenant declaration that the capacity for evil is latent in the ambitious heart and the throne will release it. Khaza'el kills Ben-Hadad the next day. Power does not transform the character — it reveals it.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 9 Yehu Anointed King Over Yisra'EL — Yehu Kills Yehoram — Yehu Kills Akhazyahu — Izevel Thrown Down

1 Then Elisha the prophet called one of the sons of the prophets and said to him, Tie up your garment, and take this flask of oil in your hand, and go to Ramot-Gil'ad.

2 And when you arrive, look there for Yehu the son of Yehoshafat, son of Nimshi. And go in and have him rise from among his brothers, and lead him to an inner chamber.

3 Then take the flask of oil and pour it on his head and say, Thus says YHWH, I anoint you king over Yisra'EL. Then open the door and flee — do not linger.

4 So the young man, the servant of the prophet, went to Ramot-Gil'ad.

5 And when he arrived, behold, the commanders of the army were in council. And he said, I have a word for you, O commander. And Yehu said, To which of us all? And he said, To you, O commander.

6 So he arose and went into the house. And the young man poured the oil on his head, saying to him, Thus says YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, I anoint you king over the people of YHWH, over Yisra'EL.

7 And you shall strike down the house of Akhav your master, so that I may avenge on Izevel the blood of my servants the prophets, and the blood of all the servants of YHWH.

8 For the whole house of Akhav shall perish, and I will cut off from Akhav every male, bond or free, in Yisra'EL.

9 And I will make the house of Akhav like the house of Yarovam the son of Nevat, and like the house of Baasha the son of Akhiyah.

10 And the dogs shall eat Izevel in the territory of Yizre'el, and none shall bury her. Then he opened the door and fled.

11 When Yehu came out to the servants of his master, they said to him, Is all well? Why did this mad fellow come to you? And he said to them, You know the fellow and his talk.

12 And they said, That is not true — tell us. And he said, Thus and so he spoke to me, saying, Thus says YHWH, I anoint you king over Yisra'EL.

13 Then in haste every man of them took his garment and put it under him on the bare steps, and they blew the trumpet and proclaimed, Yehu is king.

14 Thus Yehu the son of Yehoshafat the son of Nimshi conspired against Yehoram. Now Yehoram with all Yisra'EL had been on guard at Ramot-Gil'ad against Khaza'el king of Aram.

15 But King Yehoram had returned to be healed in Yizre'el of the wounds that the Arameans had given him, when he fought with Khaza'el king of Aram. So Yehu said, If this is your decision, let no one slip out of the city to go and tell the news in Yizre'el.

16 Then Yehu mounted his chariot and went to Yizre'el, for Yehoram lay there. And Akhazyahu king of Yehudah had come down to visit Yehoram.

17 Now the watchman was standing on the tower in Yizre'el, and he saw the company of Yehu as he came, and said, I see a company. And Yehoram said, Take a horseman and send to meet them, and let him ask, Is it peace?

18 So a man on horseback went to meet him and said, Thus says the king, Is it peace? And Yehu said, What do you have to do with peace? Turn around and ride behind me. And the watchman reported, The messenger reached them, but he is not coming back.

19 Then he sent out a second horseman, who came to them and said, Thus the king says, Is it peace? And Yehu answered, What do you have to do with peace? Turn around and ride behind me.

20 Again the watchman reported, He reached them, but he is not coming back. And the driving is like the driving of Yehu the son of Nimshi, for he drives furiously.

21 Yehoram said, Make ready. And his chariot was made ready. Then Yehoram king of Yisra'EL and Akhazyahu king of Yehudah set out, each in his chariot, and went to meet Yehu, and met him at the property of Navot the Yizreelite.

22 And when Yehoram saw Yehu, he said, Is it peace, Yehu? He answered, What peace can there be, so long as the whorings and the sorceries of your mother Izevel are so many?

23 Then Yehoram reined about and fled, saying to Akhazyahu, Treachery, O Akhazyahu.

24 And Yehu drew his bow with his full strength, and shot Yehoram between the shoulders, so that the arrow pierced his heart, and he sank in his chariot.

25 Yehu said to Bidkar his aide, Take him up and throw him on the plot of ground belonging to Navot the Yizreelite. For remember, when you and I rode side by side behind Akhav his father, how YHWH uttered this oracle against him:

26 As surely as I saw yesterday the blood of Navot and the blood of his sons — declares YHWH — I will repay you on this plot of ground. Now therefore take him up and throw him on the plot of ground, in accordance with the word of YHWH.

27 When Akhazyahu the king of Yehudah saw this, he fled in the direction of Beit-Haggan. And Yehu pursued him and said, Shoot him also. And they shot him in the chariot at the ascent of Gur, which is by Yivle'am. And he fled to Megiddo and died there.

28 His servants carried him in a chariot to Yerushalayim, and buried him in his tomb with his fathers in the city of Dawid.

29 In the eleventh year of Yehoram the son of Akhav, Akhazyahu had become king over Yehudah.

30 When Yehu came to Yizre'el, Izevel heard of it. And she painted her eyes and adorned her head and looked out of the window.

31 And as Yehu entered the gate, she said, Is it peace, you Zimri, murderer of your master?

32 And he lifted up his face to the window and said, Who is on my side? Who? Two or three eunuchs looked out at him.

33 He said, Throw her down. So they threw her down. And some of her blood spattered on the wall and on the horses, and he trampled over her.

34 Then he went in and ate and drank. And he said, See now to this cursed woman and bury her, for she is a king's daughter.

35 But when they went to bury her, they found no more of her than the skull and the feet and the palms of her hands.

36 When they came back and told him, he said, This is the word of YHWH, which he spoke by his servant Eliyahu the Tishbite: In the territory of Yizre'el the dogs shall eat the flesh of Izevel,

37 And the corpse of Izevel shall be as dung on the face of the field in the territory of Yizre'el, so that no one can say, This is Izevel.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 9 executes the covenant commission given to Eliyahu at Khorev in Melakhim Aleph 19:15-17 — three covenant judgments: Khaza'el king over Aram, Yehu king over Yisra'EL, Elisha prophet in Eliyahu's place. Yehu's anointing fulfills the second of the three. The commission spans across two prophets and multiple years.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 9 in full. Verse 26 — I saw yesterday the blood of Navot and the blood of his sons — I will repay you on this plot of ground — Yehoram dies on the very plot of Navot's vineyard. The covenant justice of YHWH returns the consequence to the exact location of the covenant crime committed by Akhav his father.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 20 — the driving is like the driving of Yehu the son of Nimshi, for he drives furiously. The covenant irony: the most recognizable thing about the new king before anyone sees his face is his driving. Yehu's furious momentum is his most characteristic covenant feature — and his most dangerous.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 22 — what peace can there be, so long as the whorings and the sorceries of your mother Izevel are so many? Yehu identifies the covenant question behind Yehoram's inquiry: peace is not a political condition but a covenant one. Where covenant whoring and sorcery govern the kingdom, peace is impossible regardless of military quietude. Verse 36-37 — the fulfillment of Eliyahu's prophecy against Izevel. The dogs ate her flesh. No one could say, this is Izevel. The covenant prophet's word, spoken years earlier in the vineyard of Navot, is fulfilled with precision. Location, instrument, outcome — all as declared.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 10 Yehu Kills the House of Akhav — Kills the Prophets of Ba'al — Yet He Did Not Turn From the Sins of Yarovam

1 Now Akhav had seventy sons in Samaria. So Yehu wrote letters and sent them to Samaria, to the rulers of the city, to the elders, and to the guardians of the sons of Akhav, saying,

2 Now then, as soon as this letter comes to you, seeing your master's sons are with you, and there are with you chariots and horses, fortified cities also, and weapons,

3 Select the best and most upright of your master's sons and set him on his father's throne and fight for your master's house.

4 But they were exceedingly afraid and said, Behold, the two kings could not stand before him. How then can we stand?

5 So he who was over the palace, and he who was over the city, together with the elders and the guardians, sent to Yehu, saying, We are your servants, and we will do all that you tell us. We will not make anyone king — do whatever is good in your eyes.

6 Then he wrote to them a second letter, saying, If you are on my side, and if you are ready to obey me, take the heads of your master's sons and come to me at Yizre'el tomorrow at this time. Now the king's sons, seventy persons, were with the great men of the city, who were bringing them up.

7 And as soon as the letter came to them, they took the king's sons and slaughtered them, seventy persons, and put their heads in baskets and sent them to him at Yizre'el.

8 When the messenger came and told him, Behold, they have brought the heads of the king's sons, he said, Lay them in two heaps at the entrance of the gate until the morning.

9 Then in the morning, when he went out, he stood and said to all the people, You are innocent. It was I who conspired against my master and killed him, but who struck down all these?

10 Know then that there shall fall to the earth nothing of the word of YHWH, which YHWH spoke concerning the house of Akhav, for YHWH has done what he said by his servant Eliyahu.

11 So Yehu struck down all who remained of the house of Akhav in Yizre'el, all his great men and his close friends and his priests, until he left him none remaining.

12 Then he set out and went to Samaria. On the way, when he was at Beit-Eked of the Shepherds,

13 Yehu met the relatives of Akhazyahu king of Yehudah, and he said, Who are you? And they answered, We are the relatives of Akhazyahu, and we came down to visit the royal princes and the sons of the queen mother.

14 He said, Take them alive. And they took them alive and slaughtered them at the pit of Beit-Eked, forty-two persons, and he left none of them.

15 And when he departed from there, he met Yehonadav the son of Rekhav coming to meet him. And he greeted him and said to him, Is your heart true to my heart as mine is to yours? And Yehonadav answered, It is. Yehu said, If it is, give me your hand. So he gave him his hand. And Yehu took him up with him into the chariot.

16 And he said, Come with me, and see my zeal for YHWH. So he had him ride in his chariot.

17 And when he came to Samaria, he struck down all who remained to Akhav in Samaria, till he had wiped them out, according to the word of YHWH that he spoke to Eliyahu.

18 Then Yehu assembled all the people and said to them, Akhav served Ba'al a little, but Yehu will serve him much.

19 Now therefore call to me all the prophets of Ba'al, all his worshipers and all his priests. Let none be missing, for I have a great sacrifice to offer to Ba'al. Whoever is missing shall not live. But Yehu did it with cunning in order to destroy the worshipers of Ba'al.

20 And Yehu ordered, Sanctify a solemn assembly for Ba'al. So they proclaimed it.

21 And Yehu sent throughout all Yisra'EL, and all the worshipers of Ba'al came, so that there was not a man left who did not come. And they entered the house of Ba'al, and the house of Ba'al was filled from one end to the other.

22 He said to him who was in charge of the wardrobe, Bring out the vestments for all the worshipers of Ba'al. So he brought out the vestments for them.

23 Then Yehu went into the house of Ba'al with Yehonadav the son of Rekhav, and he said to the worshipers of Ba'al, Search, and see that there is no servant of YHWH here among you, but only the worshipers of Ba'al.

24 Then they went in to offer sacrifices and burnt offerings. Now Yehu had stationed eighty men outside and said, The man who allows any of the men whom I give into your hands to escape shall forfeit his life.

25 As soon as he had made an end of offering the burnt offering, Yehu commanded the guard and the officers, Go in and strike them down — let not a man escape. So when they put them to the sword, the guard and the officers cast them out. And they went into the inner room of the house of Ba'al

26 And they brought out the pillar that was in the house of Ba'al and burned it.

27 And they demolished the pillar of Ba'al, and demolished the house of Ba'al, and made it a latrine to this day.

28 Thus Yehu wiped out Ba'al from Yisra'EL.

29 But Yehu did not turn aside from the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin — that is, the golden calves that were in Beit-El and in Dan.

30 And YHWH said to Yehu, Because you have done well in carrying out what is right in my eyes, and have done to the house of Akhav according to all that was in my heart, your sons of the fourth generation shall sit on the throne of Yisra'EL.

31 But Yehu was not careful to walk in the Torah of YHWH, the Creator of Yisra'EL, with all his heart. He did not turn from the sins of Yarovam, which he made Yisra'EL to sin.

32 In those days YHWH began to cut off parts of Yisra'EL. Khaza'el defeated them throughout the territory of Yisra'EL:

33 From the Yarden eastward, all the land of Gil'ad, the Gadites, and the Reuvenites, and the Menashehites, from Aro'er, which is by the Valley of the Arnon, that is, Gil'ad and Bashan.

34 Now the rest of the acts of Yehu and all that he did, and all his might, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

35 So Yehu slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria. And Yeho'ahaz his son reigned in his place.

36 The time that Yehu reigned over Yisra'EL in Samaria was twenty-eight years.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. Chapter 10 records Yehu's thoroughness in executing the covenant judgment against the house of Akhav — seventy sons killed, forty-two relatives of Akhazyahu killed, all worshipers of Ba'al assembled and slaughtered. The covenant assessment of verse 28-29 is the most precise evaluation in Melakhim: he wiped out Ba'al from Yisra'EL — but did not turn from the sins of Yarovam.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 10 in full. Verse 10 — know then that there shall fall to the earth nothing of the word of YHWH which he spoke concerning the house of Akhav — Yehu himself declares the covenant faithfulness of YHWH's word even as he is the instrument of its fulfillment.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 29-31 — the covenant summary of Yehu's reign — he wiped out Ba'al but did not turn from the golden calves of Yarovam. The partial obedience that characterizes every northern king: removing one form of covenant violation while maintaining another.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 30 — because you have done well in carrying out what is right in my eyes — your sons of the fourth generation shall sit on the throne of Yisra'EL. YHWH rewards the covenant obedience that Yehu did perform, while verse 31 documents what he did not do. The covenant evaluation is not all-or-nothing — partial faithfulness receives partial blessing. Yehu's dynasty is the longest in the northern kingdom precisely because of the covenant acts he performed. But the golden calves of Yarovam remain, and the covenant contraction of the kingdom — verse 32 — begins in his own reign.

ATALYAHU AND YO'ASH — CHAPTERS 11 THROUGH 13

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 11 Atalyahu Usurps the Throne — Yo'ash Hidden Seven Years — The Covenant Renewed — Atalyahu Executed

1 Now when Atalyahu the mother of Akhazyahu saw that her son was dead, she arose and destroyed all the royal family.

2 But Yehosheva, the daughter of King Yehoram, sister of Akhazyahu, took Yo'ash the son of Akhazyahu and stole him away from among the king's sons who were being put to death, and she put him and his nurse in a bedroom. Thus they hid him from Atalyahu, so that he was not put to death.

3 And he remained with her six years, hidden in the house of YHWH, while Atalyahu reigned over the land.

4 But in the seventh year Yehoyada sent and brought the captains of the Carites and of the guards, and had them come to him in the house of YHWH. And he made a covenant with them and put them under oath in the house of YHWH, and he showed them the king's son.

5 And he commanded them, This is the thing that you shall do: one third of you, those who come off duty on the Sabbath and guard the king's house

6 — Another third being at the gate Sur and a third at the gate behind the guards — shall guard the palace.

7 And the two divisions of you, which come on duty in force on the Sabbath and guard the house of YHWH,

8 Shall surround the king, each with his weapons in his hand. And whoever approaches the ranks is to be put to death. Be with the king when he goes out and when he comes in.

9 The captains did according to all that Yehoyada the priest commanded, and they each brought his men who were to come on duty on the Sabbath, with those who were to go off duty on the Sabbath, and came to Yehoyada the priest.

10 And the priest gave to the captains the spears and shields that had been King Dawid's, which were in the house of YHWH.

11 And the guards stood, every man with his weapons in his hand, from the south side of the house to the north side of the house, around the altar and the house, to guard the king on every side.

12 Then he brought out the king's son and put the crown on him and gave him the testimony. And they proclaimed him king and anointed him, and they clapped their hands and said, Long live the king.

13 When Atalyahu heard the noise of the guard and of the people, she went into the house of YHWH to the people.

14 And when she looked, there was the king standing by the pillar, according to the custom, with the captains and the trumpeters beside the king, and all the people of the land rejoicing and blowing trumpets. And Atalyahu tore her clothes and cried, Treason, treason.

15 Then Yehoyada the priest commanded the captains who were set over the army, Bring her out between the ranks, and put to the sword anyone who follows her. For the priest said, Let her not be put to death in the house of YHWH.

16 So they laid hands on her and she went through the horses' entrance to the king's house, and there she was put to death.

17 And Yehoyada made a covenant between YHWH and the king and people, that they should be YHWH's people, and also between the king and the people.

18 Then all the people of the land went to the house of Ba'al and tore it down. His altars and his images they broke in pieces, and they killed Mattan the priest of Ba'al before the altars. And the priest posted watchmen over the house of YHWH.

19 And he took the captains, the Carites, the guards, and all the people of the land, and they brought the king down from the house of YHWH, marching through the gate of the guards to the king's house. And he took his seat on the throne of the kings.

20 So all the people of the land rejoiced, and the city was quiet after Atalyahu had been put to death with the sword at the king's house.

21 Yo'ash was seven years old when he began to reign.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The Atalyahu crisis is the only moment in the entire Nevi'im when the Davidic line is within one generation of extinction. Yehosheva's hiding of the infant Yo'ash in the temple preserves the covenant lamp of Dawid. The seven years of Atalyahu's reign are the dark interlude between the death of Akhazyahu and the covenant restoration.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 11 in full. Verse 17 — Yehoyada made a covenant between YHWH and the king and people, that they should be YHWH's people — is read as the covenant renewal ceremony that follows every period of covenant apostasy. The new king's coronation is inseparable from the covenant renewal.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 12 — they put the crown on him and gave him the testimony — the testimony is the Torah — Devarim 17:18-20 — the covenant constitution given to the king at coronation. The Davidic king receives both the crown and the covenant document simultaneously.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 1-3 — Atalyahu destroyed all the royal family — but Yehosheva stole Yo'ash away and hid him in the house of YHWH for six years. The covenant lamp of the Davidic dynasty is preserved in YHWH's own house while a usurper sits on the throne. The irony is covenant theology: the house built for YHWH's name — the temple — becomes the hiding place for the covenant heir who will restore YHWH's covenant order. Verse 17 — Yehoyada made a covenant between YHWH and the king and people, that they should be YHWH's people, and also between the king and the people. The covenant is trilateral: YHWH, the king, and the people. Not bilateral between king and people alone.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 12 Yo'ash Repairs the Temple — The Money of the Offerings — Khaza'el Threatens Yerushalayim — Yo'ash Killed

1 In the seventh year of Yehu, Yo'ash began to reign, and he reigned forty years in Yerushalayim. His mother's name was Tzivyah of Be'er-Sheva.

2 And Yo'ash did what was right in the eyes of YHWH all his days, because Yehoyada the priest instructed him.

3 Nevertheless, the high places were not taken away — the people continued to sacrifice and make offerings on the high places.

4 Yo'ash said to the priests, All the money of the holy things that is brought into the house of YHWH, the money for which each man is assessed — the money from the assessment of persons — and the money that a man's heart prompts him to bring into the house of YHWH,

5 Let the priests take, each from his donor, and let them repair the house wherever any need of repairs is discovered.

6 But by the twenty-third year of King Yo'ash, the priests had made no repairs on the house.

7 Therefore King Yo'ash summoned Yehoyada the priest and the other priests and said to them, Why are you not repairing the house? Now therefore take no more money from your donors, but hand it over for the repair of the house.

8 So the priests agreed that they would neither accept money from the people nor repair the house.

9 Then Yehoyada the priest took a chest and bored a hole in the lid of it and set it beside the altar on the right side as one entered the house of YHWH. And the priests who guarded the threshold put in it all the money that was brought into the house of YHWH.

10 And whenever they saw that there was much money in the chest, the king's secretary and the high priest came up and they bagged and counted the money that was found in the house of YHWH.

11 Then they would give the money that was weighed out to the hands of the workmen who had the oversight of the house of YHWH. And they paid it out to the carpenters and the builders who worked on the house of YHWH,

12 And to the masons and the stonecutters, as well as to buy timber and quarried stone for making repairs on the house of YHWH, and for any outlay for the repairs of the house.

13 But there were not made for the house of YHWH basins of silver, snuffers, bowls, trumpets, or any vessels of gold, or of silver, from the money that was brought into the house of YHWH,

14 For that was given to the workmen who were repairing the house of YHWH with it.

15 And they did not ask an accounting from the men into whose hand they gave the money to pay out to the workmen, for they dealt honestly.

16 The money from the guilt offerings and the money from the sin offerings was not brought into the house of YHWH — it belonged to the priests.

17 At that time Khaza'el king of Aram went up and fought against Gat and took it. But when Khaza'el set his face to go up against Yerushalayim,

18 Yo'ash king of Yehudah took all the sacred gifts that Yehoshafat and Yehoram and Akhazyahu his fathers, the kings of Yehudah, had dedicated, and his own sacred gifts, and all the gold that was found in the treasuries of the house of YHWH and of the king's house, and sent these to Khaza'el king of Aram. Then Khaza'el went away from Yerushalayim.

19 Now the rest of the acts of Yo'ash and all that he did, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yehudah?

20 His servants arose and made a conspiracy and struck down Yo'ash in the house of Millo, on the way that goes down to Silla.

21 It was Yozakar the son of Shim'at and Yehozavad the son of Shomer, his servants, who struck him down, so that he died. And they buried him with his fathers in the city of Dawid, and Amazyahu his son reigned in his place.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The temple repair narrative of Chapter 12 is one of the few administrative accounts in Melakhim that documents covenant institutional management. The chest placed by the altar, the transparent accounting, the covenant trust in the workmen's honesty — verse 15 — are all details of covenant administrative integrity.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 12 in full. Verse 2 — Yo'ash did what was right in the eyes of YHWH all his days, because Yehoyada the priest instructed him — is read as the covenant dependence of the king on the priest for covenant formation. Yo'ash's faithfulness is not independent — it is mediated through the covenant instruction of his mentor.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 18 — Yo'ash sent all the sacred gifts to Khaza'el. The king who repaired the temple now empties it to buy off the Aramean threat. The covenant irony is precise: the gold restored to the temple through covenant faithfulness is given away under covenant pressure.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 6-8 — twenty-three years passed and the priests had made no repairs. Yo'ash's covenant administration identified the institutional failure and corrected it — removing the money from priestly discretion and routing it through a transparent chest system. The institutional reform is a model of covenant administrative wisdom. But verse 20-21 closes the chapter with Yo'ash's assassination by his own servants — the covenant king who did what was right in YHWH's eyes all his days, because Yehoyada instructed him, is killed in a conspiracy. The Divrei HaYamim account reveals that after Yehoyada's death Yo'ash turned from YHWH and even killed Yehoyada's son. The covenant faithfulness was real but dependent.

MELAKHIM BET — CHAPTER 13 Yeho'ahaz Cries to YHWH — Yoash and the Arrows — Elisha's Death — The Dead Man Revived

1 In the twenty-third year of Yo'ash the son of Akhazyahu, king of Yehudah, Yeho'ahaz the son of Yehu began to reign over Yisra'EL in Samaria, and he reigned seventeen years.

2 He did what was evil in the sight of YHWH and followed the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin — he did not depart from them.

3 And the anger of YHWH was kindled against Yisra'EL, and he gave them continually into the hand of Khaza'el king of Aram and into the hand of Ben-Hadad the son of Khaza'el.

4 Then Yeho'ahaz sought the favor of YHWH, and YHWH listened to him, for he saw the oppression of Yisra'EL, how the king of Aram oppressed them.

5 Therefore YHWH gave Yisra'EL a savior, so that they escaped from the hand of the Arameans, and the people of Yisra'EL lived in their homes as formerly.

6 Nevertheless, they did not depart from the sins of the house of Yarovam, which he made Yisra'EL to sin, but walked in them — and the Asherah also remained in Samaria.

7 For there was not left to Yeho'ahaz an army of more than fifty horsemen and ten chariots and ten thousand foot soldiers, for the king of Aram had destroyed them and made them like the dust at threshing.

8 Now the rest of the acts of Yeho'ahaz and all that he did, and his might, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

9 So Yeho'ahaz slept with his fathers, and they buried him in Samaria, and Yo'ash his son reigned in his place.

10 In the thirty-seventh year of Yo'ash king of Yehudah, Yo'ash the son of Yeho'ahaz began to reign over Yisra'EL in Samaria, and he reigned sixteen years.

11 He also did what was evil in the sight of YHWH. He did not depart from all the sins of Yarovam the son of Nevat, which he made Yisra'EL to sin, but he walked in them.

12 Now the rest of the acts of Yo'ash and all that he did, and the might with which he fought against Amazyahu king of Yehudah, are they not written in the Book of the Chronicles of the Kings of Yisra'EL?

13 So Yo'ash slept with his fathers, and Yarovam sat on his throne. And Yo'ash was buried in Samaria with the kings of Yisra'EL.

14 Now when Elisha had fallen sick with the illness of which he was to die, Yo'ash king of Yisra'EL went down to him and wept before him, crying, My father, my father. The chariots of Yisra'EL and its horsemen.

15 And Elisha said to him, Take a bow and arrows. So he took a bow and arrows.

16 Then he said to the king of Yisra'EL, Draw the bow. And he drew it. And Elisha laid his hands on the king's hands.

17 And he said, Open the window eastward. And he opened it. Then Elisha said, Shoot. And he shot. And he said, YHWH's arrow of victory, the arrow of victory over Aram, for you shall fight the Arameans in Afek until you have made an end of them.

18 And he said, Take the arrows. And he took them. And he said to the king of Yisra'EL, Strike the ground with them. And he struck three times and stopped.

19 Then the man of Creator was angry with him and said, You should have struck five or six times — then you would have struck down Aram until you had made an end of it, but now you will strike down Aram only three times.

20 So Elisha died, and they buried him. Now bands of Moavites used to invade the land in the spring of the year.

21 And as a man was being buried, behold, a marauding band was seen and the man was thrown into the grave of Elisha, and as soon as the man touched the bones of Elisha, he revived and stood on his feet.

22 Now Khaza'el king of Aram oppressed Yisra'EL all the days of Yeho'ahaz.

23 But YHWH was gracious to them and had compassion on them, and he turned toward them, because of his covenant with Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yaakov, and would not destroy them, nor has he cast them from his presence until now.

24 When Khaza'el king of Aram died, Ben-Hadad his son became king in his place.

25 Then Yo'ash the son of Yeho'ahaz took again from Ben-Hadad the son of Khaza'el the cities that he had taken from Yeho'ahaz his father in war. Three times Yo'ash defeated him and recovered the cities of Yisra'EL.

COMPARATIVE MANUSCRIPT NOTES

[DSS] No DSS manuscript of Melakhim Bet survives. The death of Elisha — and the dead man revived by touching his bones — verse 21 — is the final covenant miracle associated with Elisha. Even in death, the covenant prophet's bones carry the power of YHWH's life. The covenant life that flowed through Elisha during his ministry overflows the boundary of his death.

[ORIT GE'EZ] The Orit Ge'ez preserves Chapter 13 in full. Verse 23 — YHWH was gracious to them and had compassion on them, and he turned toward them, because of his covenant with Avraham, Yitzhak, and Yaakov — is read as the foundational covenant declaration of Melakhim: YHWH's restraint of judgment is grounded in the covenant with the patriarchs, not in the faithfulness of the kings.

[MASORETIC VARIANT] The Masoretic Text is the primary witness. Verse 14 — Yo'ash the king of Yisra'EL wept before the dying Elisha and cried, my father, my father, the chariots of Yisra'EL and its horsemen. The same cry that Elisha himself uttered when Eliyahu was taken — Chapter 2:12. The covenant succession of the prophetic word echoes through the generations.

[CRITICAL NOTE] Verse 18-19 — strike the ground with the arrows. He struck three times and stopped. Elisha was angry: you should have struck five or six times — now you will strike Aram only three times. The king's halfhearted obedience limited the covenant blessing available to him. The arrows are the covenant symbol of the promised victory — the more times he struck, the more complete the covenant fulfillment would have been. Three strikes produce three victories — enough to recover the cities — but not the decisive covenant end of the Aramean threat. The covenant principle: the measure of faith and obedience determines the measure of the covenant fulfillment.

END OF PART TWO — CHAPTERS 8 THROUGH 13
